rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Cu-catalyzed enantioselective azidocyanation of alkenes using TMSN3 and TMSCN to install azide and nitrile groups across the C=C bond
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of azide anion (N3-) to azidyl radical (N3•); Cu(I) is also anodically reoxidized to Cu(II) to close the catalytic cycle; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: beta-azido carbon radical from alkene addition
ring-opened homoallyl azide from vinylcyclopropane radical clock (48)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes N3- (generated from TMSN3/HFIP) to N3•, which adds to the alkene to give a carbon-centered radical; this radical is captured by [LCu(II)(CN)2] to form a Cu(III)-alkyl that undergoes C–CN reductive elimination, delivering the azidocyanation product and Cu(I), which is reoxidized anodically to Cu(II) while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane (47)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened azidocyanation product 48 obtained in 15% yield, consistent with a carbon-centered radical intermediate 49

probe_reagents.name: N-allyl amide alkene 50
probe_reagents.observation: Linear azidocyanation product 51 formed (45%) with no oxazoline cyclization product 52, arguing against a cationic intermediate 54 and supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: stoichiometric Cu(acac)2/L1 without electricity
probe_reagents.observation: No azidocyanation product 2 formed (0% yield, 41% conversion), showing electricity is required

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of Cu(acac)2/L1 with TMSCN and/or TMSN3
probe_reagents.observation: Trace d (with both) mirrors trace b (TMSCN) in the Cu(II/I) region, indicating [Cu–CN] dominates over [Cu–N3]; anodic peak at 0.99 V assigned to N3- → N3• oxidation appears only when TMSN3 is added

probe_reagents.name: anode potential monitoring during preparative electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Stable ~1 V potential that surges at end of reaction, consistent with depletion of N3- being oxidized at the anode

notes: Alternative cationic pathway via benzylic carbocation 54 was explicitly considered and ruled out by absence of intramolecular oxazoline cyclization product 52 from amide-tethered alkene 50. HFIP is invoked to promote H2 evolution at the cathode (suppressing undesired cathodic reduction) and to facilitate generation of free N3- from TMSN3; predominance of [Cu–CN] over [Cu–N3] suppresses competing alkene diazidation.
